title: Cancer pain review with opioid titration and carer concerns

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: How is the pain on the current morphine?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: It is still seven out of ten most afternoons. I take morphine modified release 20 mg twice a day and liquid morphine 5 mg maybe three times a day.

transcript.speaker: Carer
transcript.text: He is more sleepy after the liquid doses. I am worried about giving too much.

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: Any confusion, falls, constipation, nausea, or breathing problems?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: Constipation, yes. No breathing problems or confusion. I feel drowsy sometimes.

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: We will increase the regular dose to 30 mg twice daily, keep breakthrough at 5 mg up to hourly if needed but call if needing more than six doses in 24 hours. Start regular laxatives and call urgently for severe drowsiness, confusion, falls, or slow breathing. I will review in 48 hours.

candidate_notes.note: Palliative cancer pain review. Current morphine MR 20 mg BD plus oral morphine 5 mg breakthrough about TDS. Pain 7/10 afternoons. Carer worried about sedation. Constipation present; no confusion, falls, or respiratory depression. Plan: increase morphine MR to 30 mg BD; breakthrough morphine 5 mg up to hourly PRN, call if >6 doses/24h. Start regular laxatives. Urgent advice for severe drowsiness, confusion, falls, slow breathing. Review 48h.
Cancer pain remains high. Increase morphine to 30 mg twice daily and continue liquid morphine as needed. Constipation noted. Follow up if concerns.

S: Pain 7/10 afternoons. Taking morphine MR 20 mg BD and liquid morphine 5 mg about three times daily. Drowsiness after breakthrough. Constipation. No breathing problems/confusion. A: Inadequate analgesia with opioid adverse effects. P: Increase MR morphine to 30 mg BD. Breakthrough morphine 5 mg PRN. Laxatives. Review in 48h.

Cancer pain requiring opioid titration. Current MR morphine 20 mg BD + liquid morphine 5 mg about TDS; pain 7/10. Carer reports sedation. No confusion, falls, or slow breathing; constipation present. Increase MR morphine to 30 mg BD. Breakthrough 5 mg up to hourly PRN; call if >6 breakthrough doses/24h. Start laxatives. Urgent review for severe drowsiness, confusion, falls, or slow breathing. Review 48h.

CDSS opioid safety checklist: pain uncontrolled on morphine MR 20 mg BD plus 5 mg breakthrough about TDS. Sedation risk discussed with carer. No respiratory depression, confusion, or falls; constipation present. Titrate MR morphine to 30 mg BD, keep breakthrough morphine 5 mg up to hourly PRN, call if >6 PRN doses/day. Laxatives commenced. Red flags and 48h review documented.
